chapters.zh: 先知哈巴谷所得的默示。
chapters.en: The burden which Habakkuk the prophet did see.

chapters.zh: 他说：耶和华啊！我呼求你，你不应允，要到几时呢？我因强暴哀求你，你还不拯救。
chapters.en: O LORD, how long shall I cry, and thou wilt not hear! cry out unto thee violence, and thou wilt not save!

chapters.zh: 你为何使我看见罪孽？你为何看著奸恶而不理呢？毁灭和强暴在我面前，又起了争端和相斗的事。
chapters.en: Why dost thou shew me iniquity, and cause to behold grievance? for spoiling and violence before me: and there are raise up strife and contention.

chapters.zh: 因此律法放松，公理也不显明，恶人围困义人，所以公理显然颠倒。
chapters.en: Therefore the law is slacked, and judgment doth never go forth: for the wicked doth compass about the righteous; therefore wrong judgment proceedeth.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：你们要向列国中观看，大大惊奇；因为在你们的时候，我行一件事，虽有人告诉你们，你们总是不信。
chapters.en: Behold ye among the heathen, and regard, and wonder marvellously: for will work a work in your days, ye will not believe, though it be told .

chapters.zh: 我必兴起迦勒底人，就是那残忍暴躁之民，通行遍地，占据那不属自己的住处。
chapters.en: For, lo, I raise up the Chaldeans, bitter and hasty nation, which shall march through the breadth of the land, to possess the dwellingplaces not theirs.

chapters.zh: 他威武可畏，判断和势力都任意发出。
chapters.en: They terrible and dreadful: their judgment and their dignity shall proceed of themselves.

chapters.zh: 他的马比豹更快，比晚上的豺狼更猛。马兵踊跃争先，都从远方而来；他们飞跑如鹰抓食，
chapters.en: Their horses also are swifter than the leopards, and are more fierce than the evening wolves: and their horsemen shall spread themselves, and their horsemen shall come from far; they shall fly as the eagle hasteth to eat.

chapters.zh: 都为行强暴而来，定住脸面向前，将掳掠的人聚集，多如尘沙。
chapters.en: They shall come all for violence: their faces shall sup up the east wind, and they shall gather the captivity as the sand.

chapters.zh: 他们讥诮君王，笑话首领，嗤笑一切保障，筑垒攻取。
chapters.en: And they shall scoff at the kings, and the princes shall be a scorn unto them: they shall deride every strong hold; for they shall heap dust, and take it.

chapters.zh: 他以自己的势力为神，像风猛然扫过，显为有罪。
chapters.en: Then shall mind change, and he shall pass over, and offend, this his power unto his god.

chapters.zh: 耶和华 ─ 我的神，我的圣者啊，你不是从亘古而有么？我们必不致死。耶和华啊，你派定他为要刑罚人；磐石啊，你设立他为要惩治人。
chapters.en: thou not from everlasting, O LORD my God, mine Holy One? we shall not die. O LORD, thou hast ordained them for judgment; and, O mighty God, thou hast established them for correction.

chapters.zh: 你眼目清洁，不看邪僻，不看奸恶；行诡诈的，你为何看著不理呢？恶人吞灭比自己公义的，你为何静默不语呢？
chapters.en: of purer eyes than to behold evil, and canst not look on iniquity: wherefore lookest thou upon them that deal treacherously, holdest thy tongue when the wicked devoureth more righteous than he?

chapters.zh: 你为何使人如海中的鱼，又如没有管辖的爬物呢？
chapters.en: And makest men as the fishes of the sea, as the creeping things, no ruler over them?

chapters.zh: 他用钩钩住，用网捕获，用拉网聚集他们；因此，他欢喜快乐，
chapters.en: They take up all of them with the angle, they catch them in their net, and gather them in their drag: therefore they rejoice and are glad.

chapters.zh: 就向网献祭，向网烧香，因他由此得肥美的分和富裕的食物。
chapters.en: Therefore they sacrifice unto their net, and burn incense unto their drag; because by them their portion fat, and their meat plenteous.

chapters.zh: 他岂可屡次倒空网罗，将列国的人时常杀戮，毫不顾惜呢？
chapters.en: Shall they therefore empty their net, and not spare continually to slay the nations?

chapters.zh: 我要站在守望所，立在望楼上观看，看耶和华对我说甚么话，我可用甚么话向他诉冤（向他诉冤：或译回答所疑问的）。
chapters.en: I will stand upon my watch, and set me upon the tower, and will watch to see what he will say unto me, and what I shall answer when I am reproved.

chapters.zh: 他对我说：将这默示明明地写在版上，使读的人容易读（或译：随跑随读）。
chapters.en: And the LORD answered me, and said, Write the vision, and make plain upon tables, that he may run that readeth it.

chapters.zh: 因为这默示有一定的日期，快要应验，并不虚谎。虽然迟延，还要等候；因为必然临到，不再迟延。
chapters.en: For the vision yet for an appointed time, but at the end it shall speak, and not lie: though it tarry, wait for it; because it will surely come, it will not tarry.

chapters.zh: 迦勒底人自高自大，心不正直；惟义人因信得生。
chapters.en: Behold, his soul is lifted up is not upright in him: but the just shall live by his faith.

chapters.zh: 迦勒底人因酒诡诈，狂傲，不住在家中，扩充心欲，好像阴间。他如死不能知足，聚集万国，堆积万民都归自己。
chapters.en: Yea also, because he transgresseth by wine, a proud man, neither keepeth at home, who enlargeth his desire as hell, and as death, and cannot be satisfied, but gathereth unto him all nations, and heapeth unto him all people:

chapters.zh: 这些国的民岂不都要提起诗歌并俗语讥刺他说：祸哉！迦勒底人，你增添不属自己的财物，多多取人的当头，要到几时为止呢？
chapters.en: Shall not all these take up a parable against him, and a taunting proverb against him, and say, Woe to him that increaseth not his! how long? and to him that ladeth himself with thick clay!

chapters.zh: 咬伤你的岂不忽然起来，扰害你的岂不兴起，你就作他们的掳物么？
chapters.en: Shall they not rise up suddenly that shall bite thee, and awake that shall vex thee, and thou shalt be for booties unto them?

chapters.zh: 因你抢夺许多的国，杀人流血，向国内的城并城中一切居民施行强暴，所以各国剩下的民都必抢夺你。
chapters.en: Because thou hast spoiled many nations, all the remnant of the people shall spoil thee; because of men's blood, and the violence of the land, of the city, and of all that dwell therein.

chapters.zh: 为本家积蓄不义之财、在高处搭窝、指望免灾的有祸了！
chapters.en: Woe to him that coveteth an evil covetousness to his house, that he may set his nest on high, that he may be delivered from the power of evil!

chapters.zh: 你图谋剪除多国的民，犯了罪，使你的家蒙羞，自害己命。
chapters.en: Thou hast consulted shame to thy house by cutting off many people, and hast sinned thy soul.

chapters.zh: 墙里的石头必呼叫；房内的栋梁必应声。
chapters.en: For the stone shall cry out of the wall, and the beam out of the timber shall answer it.

chapters.zh: 以人血建城、以罪孽立邑的有祸了！
chapters.en: Woe to him that buildeth a town with blood, and stablisheth a city by iniquity!

chapters.zh: 众民所劳碌得来的被火焚烧，列国由劳乏而得的归于虚空，不都是出于万军之耶和华么？
chapters.en: Behold, not of the LORD of hosts that the people shall labour in the very fire, and the people shall weary themselves for very vanity?

chapters.zh: 认识耶和华荣耀的知识要充满遍地，好像水充满洋海一般。
chapters.en: For the earth shall be filled with the knowledge of the glory of the LORD, as the waters cover the sea.

chapters.zh: 给人酒喝、又加上毒物、使他喝醉、好看见他下体的有祸了！
chapters.en: Woe unto him that giveth his neighbour drink, that puttest thy bottle to , and makest drunken also, that thou mayest look on their nakedness!

chapters.zh: 你满受羞辱，不得荣耀；你也喝吧，显出是未受割礼的！耶和华右手的杯必传到你那里；你的荣耀就变为大大地羞辱。
chapters.en: Thou art filled with shame for glory: drink thou also, and let thy foreskin be uncovered: the cup of the LORD'S right hand shall be turned unto thee, and shameful spewing on thy glory.

chapters.zh: 你向利巴嫩行强暴与残害惊吓野兽的事必遮盖你；因你杀人流血，向国内的城并城中一切居民施行强暴。
chapters.en: For the violence of Lebanon shall cover thee, and the spoil of beasts, made them afraid, because of men's blood, and for the violence of the land, of the city, and of all that dwell therein.

chapters.zh: 雕刻的偶像，人将他刻出来，有甚么益处呢？铸造的偶像就是虚谎的师傅。制造者倚靠这哑巴偶像有甚么益处呢？
chapters.en: What profiteth the graven image that the maker thereof hath graven it; the molten image, and a teacher of lies, that the maker of his work trusteth therein, to make dumb idols?

chapters.zh: 对木偶说：醒起！对哑巴石像说：起来！那人有祸了！这个还能教训人么？看哪，是包裹金银的，其中毫无气息。
chapters.en: Woe unto him that saith to the wood, Awake; to the dumb stone, Arise, it shall teach! Behold, it laid over with gold and silver, and no breath at all in the midst of it.

chapters.zh: 惟耶和华在他的圣殿中；全地的人都当在他面前肃敬静默。
chapters.en: But the LORD in his holy temple: let all the earth keep silence before him.

chapters.zh: 先知哈巴谷的祷告，调用流离歌。
chapters.en: A prayer of Habakkuk the prophet upon Shigionoth.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，我听见你的名声（或译：言语）就惧怕。耶和华啊，求你在这些年间复兴你的作为，在这些年间显明出来；在发怒的时候以怜悯为念。
chapters.en: O LORD, I have heard thy speech, was afraid: O LORD, revive thy work in the midst of the years, in the midst of the years make known; in wrath remember mercy.

chapters.zh: 神从提幔而来；圣者从巴兰山临到。（细拉）他的荣光遮蔽诸天；颂赞充满大地。
chapters.en: God came from Teman, and the Holy One from mount Paran. Selah. His glory covered the heavens, and the earth was full of his praise.

chapters.zh: 他的辉煌如同日光；从他手里射出光线，在其中藏著他的能力。
chapters.en: And brightness was as the light; he had horns out of his hand: and there the hiding of his power.

chapters.zh: 在他前面有瘟疫流行；在他脚下有热症发出。
chapters.en: Before him went the pestilence, and burning coals went forth at his feet.

chapters.zh: 他站立，量了大地（或译：使地震动），观看，赶散万民。永久的山崩裂；长存的岭塌陷；他的作为与古时一样。
chapters.en: He stood, and measured the earth: he beheld, and drove asunder the nations; and the everlasting mountains were scattered, the perpetual hills did bow: his ways everlasting.

chapters.zh: 我见古珊的帐棚遭难，米甸的幔子战兢。
chapters.en: I saw the tents of Cushan in affliction: the curtains of the land of Midian did tremble.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你乘在马上，坐在得胜的车上，岂是不喜悦江河、向江河发怒气、向洋海发愤恨么？
chapters.en: Was the LORD displeased against the rivers? thine anger against the rivers? thy wrath against the sea, that thou didst ride upon thine horses thy chariots of salvation?

chapters.zh: 你的弓全然显露，向众支派所起的誓都是可信的。（细拉）你以江河分开大地。
chapters.en: Thy bow was made quite naked, to the oaths of the tribes, word. Selah. Thou didst cleave the earth with rivers.

chapters.zh: 山岭见你，无不战惧；大水泛滥过去，深渊发声，汹涌翻腾（原文是向上举手）。
chapters.en: The mountains saw thee, they trembled: the overflowing of the water passed by: the deep uttered his voice, lifted up his hands on high.

chapters.zh: 因你的箭射出，发光，你的枪闪出光耀，日月都在本宫停住。
chapters.en: The sun moon stood still in their habitation: at the light of thine arrows they went, at the shining of thy glittering spear.

chapters.zh: 你发愤恨通行大地，发怒气责打列国，如同打粮。
chapters.en: Thou didst march through the land in indignation, thou didst thresh the heathen in anger.

chapters.zh: 你出来要拯救你的百姓，拯救你的受膏者，打破恶人家长的头，露出他的脚（原文是根基），直到颈项。（细拉）
chapters.en: Thou wentest forth for the salvation of thy people, for salvation with thine anointed; thou woundedst the head out of the house of the wicked, by discovering the foundation unto the neck. Selah.

chapters.zh: 你用敌人的戈矛刺透他战士的头；他们来如旋风，要将我们分散。他们所喜爱的是暗中吞吃贫民。
chapters.en: Thou didst strike through with his staves the head of his villages: they came out as a whirlwind to scatter me: their rejoicing as to devour the poor secretly.

chapters.zh: 你乘马践踏红海，就是践踏汹涌的大水。
chapters.en: Thou didst walk through the sea with thine horses, the heap of great waters.

chapters.zh: 我听见耶和华的声音，身体战兢，嘴唇发颤，骨中朽烂；我在所立之处战兢。我只可安静等候灾难之日临到，犯境之民上来。
chapters.en: When I heard, my belly trembled; my lips quivered at the voice: rottenness entered into my bones, and I trembled in myself, that I might rest in the day of trouble: when he cometh up unto the people, he will invade them with his troops.

chapters.zh: 虽然无花果树不发旺，葡萄树不结果，橄榄树也不效力，田地不出粮食，圈中绝了羊，棚内也没有牛；
chapters.en: Although the fig tree shall not blossom, neither fruit in the vines; the labour of the olive shall fail, and the fields shall yield no meat; the flock shall be cut off from the fold, and no herd in the stalls:

chapters.zh: 然而，我要因耶和华欢欣，因救我的神喜乐。
chapters.en: Yet I will rejoice in the LORD, I will joy in the God of my salvation.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华是我的力量；他使我的脚快如母鹿的蹄，又使我稳行在高处。这歌交与伶长，用丝弦的乐器。
chapters.en: The LORD God my strength, and he will make my feet like hinds' , and he will make me to walk upon mine high places. To the chief singer on my stringed instruments.
